items.text: ActionLogEntry struct holds Level 1 fields: tool_name, tool_call_id, tool_use_description, timestamp, outcome, summary_line
Outcome enum with Success, Error, Blocked variants
Level 2 detail includes full tool output JSON, input arguments, resolution details, duration, and follow-up actions

Level 3 full context includes raw tool output before compaction, pre-validate and post-validate outcomes, DiagnosticsPostCheck results, caught-and-recovered errors, and before/after file content for mutation tools
Level 1 summary computed at tool-call completion and attached to the event
Level 2 structured result stored as the tool output already in EventStore — no duplication
Level 3 before-content for mutation tools captured eagerly at register_follow_ups time (FollowUpAction carries its own before-content)
Level 3 for non-mutation tools populated lazily on first context query
action_log tool registered with query parameter accepting list, detail, context, mutations, follow_ups values
filter object supports tool (tool name), outcome (success/error/blocked), file (affected file path), since (tool_call_id), and last (count) fields
list query returns Level 1 summaries matching the filter
detail query requires call_id parameter and returns Level 2 for that specific tool call
context query requires call_id parameter and returns Level 3 for that specific tool call
follow_ups query returns follow-up actions matching the filter with expired actions filtered out at query time
mutations query returns mutation ledger entries (see Mutation Ledger section)
Invalid query values or missing required parameters (call_id for detail/context) return structured errors
Follow-up actions stored on action log entries with action_id, description, tool, args, expires, and confidence fields
Follow-up actions indexed by tool_call_id + action name for O(1) lookup
follow_ups query returns only unexpired follow-up actions — expired ones filtered out at query time
Expiry checking at query time compares file modification timestamp or content hash against stored value
MutationLedgerEntry struct tracks file_path, operation (created/modified/deleted), first_tool_call_id, last_tool_call_id, revert_status, and diff_stats (lines added/removed)
Mutation ledger is a derived view over action log entries — not a separate store
External revert detection: at query time, hash each entry's file and compare against post-mutation hash recorded at tool-call completion
If file hash differs and no subsequent tool call in the log touched that file, entry marked externally_reverted
No filesystem watching or polling — detection only at query time
Mutation ledger shows only mutations from the current session's tool calls, not pre-existing dirty files or changes from other agents
Action log data stored as events in the session's EventStore — no new storage backend
Structured metadata (Level 1 summary, follow-up actions) attached to existing tool-call events
Storage uses existing EventStore and PersistenceSink exclusively — no new database or file format
Action log retains full data regardless of context compaction state
Agent can drill into any prior tool call via detail or context query even if the tool result was compacted from conversation
action_log tool output is compact by design — Level 1 is a few tokens per entry, safe to call frequently without bloating context
Level 1 list query for 100 tool calls produces under 1000 tokens of output (CO1)
Follow-up action expiry checked at query time, not maintained in real-time (CO2)
Level 3 file snapshots for mutation tools are opt-in or lazily populated — not eagerly stored for every edit (CO3)
action_log tool queries logged at Level 1 only — no Level 2/3 self-referential entries that create recursive noise (CO4)
No new database or file format — storage via existing EventStore and PersistenceSink only (CO5)
ToolError::PostValidationFailed carries an optional committed_output field so that action log entries for failed-but-committed mutations include the structured output (PR0)
